content.thinking: Now let me create the setup script for Windows.

input.content:
# Claude Code Project Setup Script
# Usage: ./setup.ps1 -ProjectPath "C:\path\to\project" [-SymlinkSkills]

param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory=$true)]
    [string]$ProjectPath,

    [switch]$SymlinkSkills,

    [switch]$Force
)

$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
$GlobalClaudeDir = "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"
$TemplateDir = "$GlobalClaudeDir\portable-template"

# Resolve project path
$ProjectPath = Resolve-Path $ProjectPath -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if (-not $ProjectPath) {
    Write-Error "Project path does not exist"
    exit 1
}

$ProjectClaudeDir = Join-Path $ProjectPath ".claude"
$ProjectClaudeMd = Join-Path $ProjectPath "CLAUDE.md"

Write-Host "Setting up Claude Code for: $ProjectPath" -ForegroundColor Cyan

# Check for existing .claude folder
if (Test-Path $ProjectClaudeDir) {
    if ($Force) {
        Write-Host "Removing existing .claude folder (--Force)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $ProjectClaudeDir
    } else {
        Write-Error ".claude folder already exists. Use -Force to overwrite."
        exit 1
    }
}

# Create .claude structure
Write-Host "Creating .claude directory structure..." -ForegroundColor Green
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $ProjectClaudeDir -Force | Out-Null
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$ProjectClaudeDir\commands" -Force | Out-Null
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$ProjectClaudeDir\templates" -Force | Out-Null

# Copy commands
Write-Host "Copying slash commands..." -ForegroundColor Green
Copy-Item -Path "$GlobalClaudeDir\commands\*" -Destination "$ProjectClaudeDir\commands\" -Recurse

# Handle skills (symlink or copy)
if ($SymlinkSkills) {
    Write-Host "Creating symlink to global skills..." -ForegroundColor Green
    New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path "$ProjectClaudeDir\skills" -Target "$GlobalClaudeDir\skills" | Out-Null
} else {
    Write-Host "Copying skills (use -SymlinkSkills to share updates)..." -ForegroundColor Green
    Copy-Item -Path "$GlobalClaudeDir\skills" -Destination "$ProjectClaudeDir\" -Recurse
}

# Copy templates
if (Test-Path "$GlobalClaudeDir\templates") {
    Write-Host "Copying templates..." -ForegroundColor Green
    Copy-Item -Path "$GlobalClaudeDir\templates\*" -Destination "$ProjectClaudeDir\templates\" -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
}

# Create minimal settings.json
$settingsContent = @"
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Bash(npm run:*)",
      "Bash(git:*)",
      "WebSearch"
    ],
    "deny": [],
    "defaultMode": "default"
  },
  "alwaysThinkingEnabled": true,
  "spinnerTipsEnabled": false
}
"@
Set-Content -Path "$ProjectClaudeDir\settings.json" -Value $settingsContent

# Copy or create CLAUDE.md
if (-not (Test-Path $ProjectClaudeMd)) {
    if (Test-Path "$TemplateDir\CLAUDE.md") {
        Write-Host "Copying CLAUDE.md template..." -ForegroundColor Green
        Copy-Item -Path "$TemplateDir\CLAUDE.md" -Destination $ProjectClaudeMd
    } else {
        Write-Host "Creating minimal CLAUDE.md..." -ForegroundColor Green
        $claudeMdContent = @"
# Project Name

> Brief project description

## Tech Stack
-

## Key Conventions
-

## Directory Structure
```
src/
```

## Commands
```bash
npm run dev      # Start dev server
npm run build    # Production build
npm run test     # Run tests
```
"@
        Set-Content -Path $ProjectClaudeMd -Value $claudeMdContent
    }
} else {
    Write-Host "CLAUDE.md already exists, skipping..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
}

Write-Host "`nSetup complete!" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "Files created:"
Write-Host "  - $ProjectClaudeDir\commands\"
Write-Host "  - $ProjectClaudeDir\skills\"
Write-Host "  - $ProjectClaudeDir\templates\"
Write-Host "  - $ProjectClaudeDir\settings.json"
if (-not (Test-Path $ProjectClaudeMd -PathType Leaf)) {
    Write-Host "  - $ProjectClaudeMd"
}
Write-Host "`nNext steps:"
Write-Host "  1. Edit CLAUDE.md with project-specific details"
Write-Host "  2. Run 'claude' in the project directory"

input.content:
@echo off
REM Claude Code Project Setup (Simple Version)
REM Usage: setup.bat [project-path]
REM If no path provided, uses current directory

setlocal

if "%~1"=="" (
    set "PROJECT_PATH=%CD%"
) else (
    set "PROJECT_PATH=%~1"
)

set "GLOBAL_CLAUDE=%USERPROFILE%\.claude"
set "PROJECT_CLAUDE=%PROJECT_PATH%\.claude"

echo Setting up Claude Code for: %PROJECT_PATH%

if exist "%PROJECT_CLAUDE%" (
    echo WARNING: .claude folder already exists
    set /p CONFIRM="Overwrite? (y/N): "
    if /i not "%CONFIRM%"=="y" exit /b 1
    rmdir /s /q "%PROJECT_CLAUDE%"
)

echo Creating .claude structure...
mkdir "%PROJECT_CLAUDE%\commands" 2>nul
mkdir "%PROJECT_CLAUDE%\templates" 2>nul

echo Copying commands...
xcopy /E /I /Y "%GLOBAL_CLAUDE%\commands" "%PROJECT_CLAUDE%\commands"

echo Copying skills...
xcopy /E /I /Y "%GLOBAL_CLAUDE%\skills" "%PROJECT_CLAUDE%\skills"

echo Copying templates...
if exist "%GLOBAL_CLAUDE%\templates" (
    xcopy /E /I /Y "%GLOBAL_CLAUDE%\templates" "%PROJECT_CLAUDE%\templates"
)

echo Copying settings template...
copy /Y "%GLOBAL_CLAUDE%\portable-template\.claude\settings.json" "%PROJECT_CLAUDE%\settings.json"

if not exist "%PROJECT_PATH%\CLAUDE.md" (
    echo Copying CLAUDE.md template...
    copy /Y "%GLOBAL_CLAUDE%\portable-template\CLAUDE.md" "%PROJECT_PATH%\CLAUDE.md"
) else (
    echo CLAUDE.md already exists, skipping...
)

echo.
echo Setup complete!
echo.
echo Next steps:
echo   1. Edit CLAUDE.md with project-specific details
echo   2. Run 'claude' in the project directory

endlocal

input.content:
# ~/.claude/ Directory Structure

This folder contains Claude Code CLI configuration, skills, commands, and session data.

---

## Folder Hierarchy

```
.claude/
├── CLAUDE.md                 # Global instructions (applies to all projects)
├── README.md                 # This file
├── settings.json             # Global settings (permissions, plugins, preferences)
├── settings.local.json       # Local settings override
│
├── commands/                 # Custom slash commands
│   ├── run.md               # /run command
│   └── start-task.md        # /start-task command
│
├── skills/                   # Reusable skills invoked via Skill tool
│   ├── _shared/             # Shared standards across skills
│   ├── generic-*/           # Stack-agnostic skills
│   ├── codebase-documenter/ # Documentation generation
│   ├── frontend-enhancer/   # UI/UX improvements
│   ├── tech-debt-analyzer/  # Technical debt analysis
│   ├── test-specialist/     # Testing & debugging
│   └── ...                  # Additional skills
│
├── templates/                # Task and document templates
├── docs/                     # Documentation files
│
├── CLAUDE.md Reference/      # Reference CLAUDE.md files from other projects
│
│── ─── DO NOT COPY BELOW THIS LINE ─────────────────────────────
│
├── .claude/                  # Internal Claude Code state
│   └── settings.local.json  # Machine-local settings
│
├── .claude.json              # Auth tokens, MCP servers, account info
├── .credentials.json         # OAuth credentials
│
├── history.jsonl             # Conversation history (can be large)
├── file-history/             # File change tracking
├── shell-snapshots/          # Bash shell state persistence
│
├── plans/                    # Session-specific plan files
├── todos/                    # Session todo states
├── debug/                    # Debug logs
│
├── projects/                 # Per-project state (path-encoded folders)
├── plugins/                  # Downloaded plugin cache
│   └── cache/               # Auto-regenerated on plugin install
│
├── statsig/                  # Feature flags cache
├── stats-cache.json          # Usage statistics
├── telemetry/                # Telemetry data
│
├── ide/                      # IDE connection state
├── downloads/                # Temporary downloads
└── sqlite_mcp_server.db      # MCP SQLite database
```

---

## What to Copy vs. What NOT to Copy

### ✅ COPY THESE (Portable)

| Folder/File | Purpose |
|-------------|---------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | Global instructions and conventions |
| `commands/` | Custom slash commands |
| `skills/` | Reusable skills with references and scripts |
| `templates/` | Task and document templates |
| `docs/` | Documentation |
| `settings.json` | Permissions and plugin config (review for machine-specific paths) |
| `CLAUDE.md Reference/` | Optional - reference files from other projects |

### ❌ DO NOT COPY (Machine-Specific)

| Folder/File | Why |
|-------------|-----|
| `.claude.json` | Contains OAuth tokens, account UUID, MCP server config |
| `.credentials.json` | Authentication credentials |
| `.claude/` subfolder | Internal state with local settings |
| `history.jsonl` | Conversation history (can be multiple MB) |
| `file-history/` | File change tracking per session |
| `shell-snapshots/` | Bash shell state |
| `plans/` | Session-specific plan files |
| `todos/` | Session todo states |
| `debug/` | Debug logs |
| `projects/` | Per-project state keyed by path |
| `plugins/cache/` | Downloaded plugins (auto-regenerates) |
| `statsig/` | Feature flags cache |
| `stats-cache.json` | Usage statistics |
| `telemetry/` | Telemetry data |
| `ide/` | IDE connection state |
| `downloads/` | Temporary files |
| `sqlite_mcp_server.db` | MCP database |

---

## Key Files Explained

### CLAUDE.md
Global instructions that apply to ALL projects. This is the "constitution" for Claude's behavior. Project-specific CLAUDE.md files in individual repos override or extend these.

### settings.json
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": ["Bash(npm run:*)", "WebSearch"],
    "deny": [],
    "defaultMode": "default"  // or "bypassPermissions"
  },
  "enabledPlugins": { ... },
  "alwaysThinkingEnabled": true
}
```

### commands/
Markdown files that define custom slash commands. File name becomes command name:
- `run.md` → `/run`
- `start-task.md` → `/start-task`

### skills/
Each skill folder contains:
- `SKILL.md` - Skill definition and instructions
- `references/` - Supporting documentation
- `scripts/` - Executable scripts (Python, JS)
- `assets/` - Templates, examples

---

## Copying to a New Machine

```bash
# Copy portable assets only
rsync -av --exclude='.claude.json' \
          --exclude='.credentials.json' \
          --exclude='.claude/' \
          --exclude='history.jsonl' \
          --exclude='file-history/' \
          --exclude='shell-snapshots/' \
          --exclude='plans/' \
          --exclude='todos/' \
          --exclude='debug/' \
          --exclude='projects/' \
          --exclude='plugins/' \
          --exclude='statsig/' \
          --exclude='stats-cache.json' \
          --exclude='telemetry/' \
          --exclude='ide/' \
          --exclude='downloads/' \
          --exclude='*.db' \
          ~/.claude/ /path/to/new/location/.claude/
```

Or on Windows (PowerShell):
```powershell
$exclude = @('.claude.json', '.credentials.json', '.claude', 'history.jsonl',
             'file-history', 'shell-snapshots', 'plans', 'todos', 'debug',
             'projects', 'plugins', 'statsig', 'stats-cache.json',
             'telemetry', 'ide', 'downloads', '*.db')

robocopy "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude" "D:\new-location\.claude" /E /XF $exclude /XD $exclude
```

---

## After Copying

1. **Login**: Run `claude` - you'll need to authenticate on the new machine
2. **MCP Servers**: Check `.claude.json` for MCP config - may need Windows `cmd /c` wrappers
3. **Review settings.json**: Remove any machine-specific paths
4. **Install plugins**: Run `/plugin install` for any plugins you need

---

## Maintenance

| Task | Frequency |
|------|-----------|
| Clear `plans/` | Monthly (session-specific, safe to delete) |
| Clear `history.jsonl` | As needed (can grow to several MB) |
| Clear `debug/` | As needed |
| Update skills | When new versions available |
| Review CLAUDE.md | Quarterly or after major project changes |

---

## Plugin Marketplaces

Plugins are installed from marketplaces and cached in `plugins/cache/`. Current enabled:
- `claude-code-plugins` - Official Anthropic plugins
- `anthropic-agent-skills` - Document skills (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX)
- `taches-cc-resources` - Community resources

---

*Last updated: December 2024*

- `sqlite_mcp_server.db` - Database file that might have locks or be corrupted if copied mid-use

- `file-history/` - File change tracking, harmless

- `telemetry/` - Telemetry data, harmless
- `ide/` - IDE connection state, harmless (will reconnect)
- `downloads/` - Temp files, harmless
- `.claude/` subfolder - Local settings, harmless

file.content:
# ~/.claude/ Directory Structure

This folder contains Claude Code CLI configuration, skills, commands, and session data.

---

## Folder Hierarchy

```
.claude/
├── CLAUDE.md                 # Global instructions (applies to all projects)
├── README.md                 # This file
├── settings.json             # Global settings (permissions, plugins, preferences)
├── settings.local.json       # Local settings override
│
├── commands/                 # Custom slash commands
│   ├── run.md               # /run command
│   └── start-task.md        # /start-task command
│
├── skills/                   # Reusable skills invoked via Skill tool
│   ├── _shared/             # Shared standards across skills
│   ├── generic-*/           # Stack-agnostic skills
│   ├── codebase-documenter/ # Documentation generation
│   ├── frontend-enhancer/   # UI/UX improvements
│   ├── tech-debt-analyzer/  # Technical debt analysis
│   ├── test-specialist/     # Testing & debugging
│   └── ...                  # Additional skills
│
├── templates/                # Task and document templates
├── docs/                     # Documentation files
│
├── CLAUDE.md Reference/      # Reference CLAUDE.md files from other projects
│
│── ─── DO NOT COPY BELOW THIS LINE ─────────────────────────────
│
├── .claude/                  # Internal Claude Code state
│   └── settings.local.json  # Machine-local settings
│
├── .claude.json              # Auth tokens, MCP servers, account info
├── .credentials.json         # OAuth credentials
│
├── history.jsonl             # Conversation history (can be large)
├── file-history/             # File change tracking
├── shell-snapshots/          # Bash shell state persistence
│
├── plans/                    # Session-specific plan files
├── todos/                    # Session todo states
├── debug/                    # Debug logs
│
├── projects/                 # Per-project state (path-encoded folders)
├── plugins/                  # Downloaded plugin cache
│   └── cache/               # Auto-regenerated on plugin install
│
├── statsig/                  # Feature flags cache
├── stats-cache.json          # Usage statistics
├── telemetry/                # Telemetry data
│
├── ide/                      # IDE connection state
├── downloads/                # Temporary downloads
└── sqlite_mcp_server.db      # MCP SQLite database
```

---

## What to Copy vs. What NOT to Copy

### ✅ COPY THESE (Portable)

| Folder/File | Purpose |
|-------------|---------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | Global instructions and conventions |
| `commands/` | Custom slash commands |
| `skills/` | Reusable skills with references and scripts |
| `templates/` | Task and document templates |
| `docs/` | Documentation |
| `settings.json` | Permissions and plugin config (review for machine-specific paths) |
| `CLAUDE.md Reference/` | Optional - reference files from other projects |

### ❌ DO NOT COPY (Machine-Specific)

| Folder/File | Why |
|-------------|-----|
| `.claude.json` | Contains OAuth tokens, account UUID, MCP server config |
| `.credentials.json` | Authentication credentials |
| `.claude/` subfolder | Internal state with local settings |
| `history.jsonl` | Conversation history (can be multiple MB) |
| `file-history/` | File change tracking per session |
| `shell-snapshots/` | Bash shell state |
| `plans/` | Session-specific plan files |
| `todos/` | Session todo states |
| `debug/` | Debug logs |
| `projects/` | Per-project state keyed by path |
| `plugins/cache/` | Downloaded plugins (auto-regenerates) |
| `statsig/` | Feature flags cache |
| `stats-cache.json` | Usage statistics |
| `telemetry/` | Telemetry data |
| `ide/` | IDE connection state |
| `downloads/` | Temporary files |
| `sqlite_mcp_server.db` | MCP database |

---

## Key Files Explained

### CLAUDE.md
Global instructions that apply to ALL projects. This is the "constitution" for Claude's behavior. Project-specific CLAUDE.md files in individual repos override or extend these.

### settings.json
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": ["Bash(npm run:*)", "WebSearch"],
    "deny": [],
    "defaultMode": "default"  // or "bypassPermissions"
  },
  "enabledPlugins": { ... },
  "alwaysThinkingEnabled": true
}
```

### commands/
Markdown files that define custom slash commands. File name becomes command name:
- `run.md` → `/run`
- `start-task.md` → `/start-task`

### skills/
Each skill folder contains:
- `SKILL.md` - Skill definition and instructions
- `references/` - Supporting documentation
- `scripts/` - Executable scripts (Python, JS)
- `assets/` - Templates, examples

---

## Copying to a New Machine

```bash
# Copy portable assets only
rsync -av --exclude='.claude.json' \
          --exclude='.credentials.json' \
          --exclude='.claude/' \
          --exclude='history.jsonl' \
          --exclude='file-history/' \
          --exclude='shell-snapshots/' \
          --exclude='plans/' \
          --exclude='todos/' \
          --exclude='debug/' \
          --exclude='projects/' \
          --exclude='plugins/' \
          --exclude='statsig/' \
          --exclude='stats-cache.json' \
          --exclude='telemetry/' \
          --exclude='ide/' \
          --exclude='downloads/' \
          --exclude='*.db' \
          ~/.claude/ /path/to/new/location/.claude/
```

Or on Windows (PowerShell):
```powershell
$exclude = @('.claude.json', '.credentials.json', '.claude', 'history.jsonl',
             'file-history', 'shell-snapshots', 'plans', 'todos', 'debug',
             'projects', 'plugins', 'statsig', 'stats-cache.json',
             'telemetry', 'ide', 'downloads', '*.db')

robocopy "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude" "D:\new-location\.claude" /E /XF $exclude /XD $exclude
```

---

## After Copying

1. **Login**: Run `claude` - you'll need to authenticate on the new machine
2. **MCP Servers**: Check `.claude.json` for MCP config - may need Windows `cmd /c` wrappers
3. **Review settings.json**: Remove any machine-specific paths
4. **Install plugins**: Run `/plugin install` for any plugins you need

---

## Maintenance

| Task | Frequency |
|------|-----------|
| Clear `plans/` | Monthly (session-specific, safe to delete) |
| Clear `history.jsonl` | As needed (can grow to several MB) |
| Clear `debug/` | As needed |
| Update skills | When new versions available |
| Review CLAUDE.md | Quarterly or after major project changes |

---

## Plugin Marketplaces

Plugins are installed from marketplaces and cached in `plugins/cache/`. Current enabled:
- `claude-code-plugins` - Official Anthropic plugins
- `anthropic-agent-skills` - Document skills (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX)
- `taches-cc-resources` - Community resources

---

*Last updated: December 2024*

## What to Copy vs. What NOT to Copy

### ✅ COPY EVERYTHING EXCEPT:

| Folder/File | Why NOT to Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `.claude.json` | Contains OAuth tokens tied to your account and MCP server paths with machine-specific locations. Will be regenerated on login. |

### ✅ SAFE TO COPY (Everything Else)

All other files and folders are safe to copy. They may contain session-specific data that gets overwritten, but they won't cause errors:

| Folder/File | Notes |
|-------------|-------|
| `history.jsonl` | Conversation history - can be large but harmless |
| `plans/`, `todos/` | Session state - will be replaced as you work |
| `plugins/cache/` | Downloaded plugins - will be reused or regenerated |
| `statsig/`, `telemetry/` | Analytics data - harmless |
| `debug/`, `downloads/` | Temp files - harmless |
| `shell-snapshots/`, `file-history/` | Session state - harmless |
| `ide/` | IDE connection - will reconnect |
| `sqlite_mcp_server.db` | MCP database - empty or will be recreated |
| `.claude/` subfolder | Local settings - harmless |

## What to Copy vs. What NOT to Copy

### ✅ COPY EVERYTHING EXCEPT:

| Folder/File | Why NOT to Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `.claude.json` | Contains OAuth tokens tied to your account and MCP server paths with machine-specific locations. Will be regenerated on login. |
| `.credentials.json` | OAuth credentials for this machine. Will be regenerated on login. |
| `projects/` | Folders are keyed by absolute path (e.g., `C--Users-tjn--claude`). These paths won't exist on other machines and will cause confusion. |

### ✅ SAFE TO COPY (Everything Else)

All other files and folders are safe to copy. They may contain session-specific data that gets overwritten, but they won't cause errors:

| Folder/File | Notes |
|-------------|-------|
| `history.jsonl` | Conversation history - can be large but harmless |
| `plans/`, `todos/` | Session state - will be replaced as you work |
| `plugins/cache/` | Downloaded plugins - will be reused or regenerated |
| `statsig/`, `telemetry/` | Analytics data - harmless |
| `debug/`, `downloads/` | Temp files - harmless |
| `shell-snapshots/`, `file-history/` | Session state - harmless |
| `ide/` | IDE connection - will reconnect |
| `sqlite_mcp_server.db` | MCP database - empty or will be recreated |
| `.claude/` subfolder | Local settings - harmless |
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ~/.claude/ Directory Structure

This folder contains Claude Code CLI configuration, skills, commands, and session data.

---

## Folder Hierarchy

```
.claude/
├── CLAUDE.md                 # Global instructions (applies to all projects)
├── README.md                 # This file
├── settings.json             # Global settings (permissions, plugins, preferences)
├── settings.local.json       # Local settings override
│
├── commands/                 # Custom slash commands
│   ├── run.md               # /run command
│   └── start-task.md        # /start-task command
│
├── skills/                   # Reusable skills invoked via Skill tool
│   ├── _shared/             # Shared standards across skills
│   ├── generic-*/           # Stack-agnostic skills
│   ├── codebase-documenter/ # Documentation generation
│   ├── frontend-enhancer/   # UI/UX improvements
│   ├── tech-debt-analyzer/  # Technical debt analysis
│   ├── test-specialist/     # Testing & debugging
│   └── ...                  # Additional skills
│
├── templates/                # Task and document templates
├── docs/                     # Documentation files
│
├── CLAUDE.md Reference/      # Reference CLAUDE.md files from other projects
│
├── .claude/                  # Internal Claude Code state
│   └── settings.local.json  # Machine-local settings
│
├── .claude.json              # ⚠️ DO NOT COPY - Auth tokens, MCP paths
├── .credentials.json         # ⚠️ DO NOT COPY - OAuth credentials
│
├── history.jsonl             # Conversation history (can be large)
├── file-history/             # File change tracking
├── shell-snapshots/          # Bash shell state persistence
│
├── plans/                    # Session-specific plan files
├── todos/                    # Session todo states
├── debug/                    # Debug logs
│
├── projects/                 # ⚠️ DO NOT COPY - Path-encoded folders
├── plugins/                  # Downloaded plugin cache
│   └── cache/               # Auto-regenerated on plugin install
│
├── statsig/                  # Feature flags cache
├── stats-cache.json          # Usage statistics
├── telemetry/                # Telemetry data
│
├── ide/                      # IDE connection state
├── downloads/                # Temporary downloads
└── sqlite_mcp_server.db      # MCP SQLite database
```

---

## What to Copy vs. What NOT to Copy

### ✅ COPY THESE (Portable)

| Folder/File | Purpose |
|-------------|---------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | Global instructions and conventions |
| `commands/` | Custom slash commands |
| `skills/` | Reusable skills with references and scripts |
| `templates/` | Task and document templates |
| `docs/` | Documentation |
| `settings.json` | Permissions and plugin config (review for machine-specific paths) |
| `CLAUDE.md Reference/` | Optional - reference files from other projects |

### ❌ DO NOT COPY (Machine-Specific)

| Folder/File | Why |
|-------------|-----|
| `.claude.json` | Contains OAuth tokens, account UUID, MCP server config |
| `.credentials.json` | Authentication credentials |
| `.claude/` subfolder | Internal state with local settings |
| `history.jsonl` | Conversation history (can be multiple MB) |
| `file-history/` | File change tracking per session |
| `shell-snapshots/` | Bash shell state |
| `plans/` | Session-specific plan files |
| `todos/` | Session todo states |
| `debug/` | Debug logs |
| `projects/` | Per-project state keyed by path |
| `plugins/cache/` | Downloaded plugins (auto-regenerates) |
| `statsig/` | Feature flags cache |
| `stats-cache.json` | Usage statistics |
| `telemetry/` | Telemetry data |
| `ide/` | IDE connection state |
| `downloads/` | Temporary files |
| `sqlite_mcp_server.db` | MCP database |

---

## Key Files Explained

### CLAUDE.md
Global instructions that apply to ALL projects. This is the "constitution" for Claude's behavior. Project-specific CLAUDE.md files in individual repos override or extend these.

### settings.json
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": ["Bash(npm run:*)", "WebSearch"],
    "deny": [],
    "defaultMode": "default"  // or "bypassPermissions"
  },
  "enabledPlugins": { ... },
  "alwaysThinkingEnabled": true
}
```

### commands/
Markdown files that define custom slash commands. File name becomes command name:
- `run.md` → `/run`
- `start-task.md` → `/start-task`

### skills/
Each skill folder contains:
- `SKILL.md` - Skill definition and instructions
- `references/` - Supporting documentation
- `scripts/` - Executable scripts (Python, JS)
- `assets/` - Templates, examples

---

## Copying to a New Machine

```bash
# Copy portable assets only
rsync -av --exclude='.claude.json' \
          --exclude='.credentials.json' \
          --exclude='.claude/' \
          --exclude='history.jsonl' \
          --exclude='file-history/' \
          --exclude='shell-snapshots/' \
          --exclude='plans/' \
          --exclude='todos/' \
          --exclude='debug/' \
          --exclude='projects/' \
          --exclude='plugins/' \
          --exclude='statsig/' \
          --exclude='stats-cache.json' \
          --exclude='telemetry/' \
          --exclude='ide/' \
          --exclude='downloads/' \
          --exclude='*.db' \
          ~/.claude/ /path/to/new/location/.claude/
```

Or on Windows (PowerShell):
```powershell
$exclude = @('.claude.json', '.credentials.json', '.claude', 'history.jsonl',
             'file-history', 'shell-snapshots', 'plans', 'todos', 'debug',
             'projects', 'plugins', 'statsig', 'stats-cache.json',
             'telemetry', 'ide', 'downloads', '*.db')

robocopy "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude" "D:\new-location\.claude" /E /XF $exclude /XD $exclude
```

---

## After Copying

1. **Login**: Run `claude` - you'll need to authenticate on the new machine
2. **MCP Servers**: Check `.claude.json` for MCP config - may need Windows `cmd /c` wrappers
3. **Review settings.json**: Remove any machine-specific paths
4. **Install plugins**: Run `/plugin install` for any plugins you need

---

## Maintenance

| Task | Frequency |
|------|-----------|
| Clear `plans/` | Monthly (session-specific, safe to delete) |
| Clear `history.jsonl` | As needed (can grow to several MB) |
| Clear `debug/` | As needed |
| Update skills | When new versions available |
| Review CLAUDE.md | Quarterly or after major project changes |

---

## Plugin Marketplaces

Plugins are installed from marketplaces and cached in `plugins/cache/`. Current enabled:
- `claude-code-plugins` - Official Anthropic plugins
- `anthropic-agent-skills` - Document skills (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX)
- `taches-cc-resources` - Community resources

---

*Last updated: December 2024*

## Copying to a New Machine

### Linux/Mac (rsync)
```bash

          --exclude='.credentials.json' \
          --exclude='projects/' \
          ~/.claude/ /path/to/new/location/.claude/

```cmd

```
Where `exclude.txt` contains:

.claude.json
.credentials.json
\projects\
```

# ~/.claude/ Directory Structure

This folder contains Claude Code CLI configuration, skills, commands, and session data.

---

## Folder Hierarchy

```
.claude/
├── CLAUDE.md                 # Global instructions (applies to all projects)
├── README.md                 # This file
├── settings.json             # Global settings (permissions, plugins, preferences)
├── settings.local.json       # Local settings override
│
├── commands/                 # Custom slash commands
│   ├── run.md               # /run command
│   └── start-task.md        # /start-task command
│
├── skills/                   # Reusable skills invoked via Skill tool
│   ├── _shared/             # Shared standards across skills
│   ├── generic-*/           # Stack-agnostic skills
│   ├── codebase-documenter/ # Documentation generation
│   ├── frontend-enhancer/   # UI/UX improvements
│   ├── tech-debt-analyzer/  # Technical debt analysis
│   ├── test-specialist/     # Testing & debugging
│   └── ...                  # Additional skills
│
├── templates/                # Task and document templates
├── docs/                     # Documentation files
│
├── CLAUDE.md Reference/      # Reference CLAUDE.md files from other projects
│
├── .claude/                  # Internal Claude Code state
│   └── settings.local.json  # Machine-local settings
│
├── .claude.json              # ⚠️ DO NOT COPY - Auth tokens, MCP paths
├── .credentials.json         # ⚠️ DO NOT COPY - OAuth credentials
│
├── history.jsonl             # Conversation history (can be large)
├── file-history/             # File change tracking
├── shell-snapshots/          # Bash shell state persistence
│
├── plans/                    # Session-specific plan files
├── todos/                    # Session todo states
├── debug/                    # Debug logs
│
├── projects/                 # ⚠️ DO NOT COPY - Path-encoded folders
├── plugins/                  # Downloaded plugin cache
│   └── cache/               # Auto-regenerated on plugin install
│
├── statsig/                  # Feature flags cache
├── stats-cache.json          # Usage statistics
├── telemetry/                # Telemetry data
│
├── ide/                      # IDE connection state
├── downloads/                # Temporary downloads
└── sqlite_mcp_server.db      # MCP SQLite database
```

---

## What to Copy vs. What NOT to Copy

### ✅ COPY EVERYTHING EXCEPT:

| Folder/File | Why NOT to Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `.claude.json` | Contains OAuth tokens tied to your account and MCP server paths with machine-specific locations. Will be regenerated on login. |
| `.credentials.json` | OAuth credentials for this machine. Will be regenerated on login. |
| `projects/` | Folders are keyed by absolute path (e.g., `C--Users-tjn--claude`). These paths won't exist on other machines and will cause confusion. |

### ✅ SAFE TO COPY (Everything Else)

All other files and folders are safe to copy. They may contain session-specific data that gets overwritten, but they won't cause errors:

| Folder/File | Notes |
|-------------|-------|
| `history.jsonl` | Conversation history - can be large but harmless |
| `plans/`, `todos/` | Session state - will be replaced as you work |
| `plugins/cache/` | Downloaded plugins - will be reused or regenerated |
| `statsig/`, `telemetry/` | Analytics data - harmless |
| `debug/`, `downloads/` | Temp files - harmless |
| `shell-snapshots/`, `file-history/` | Session state - harmless |
| `ide/` | IDE connection - will reconnect |
| `sqlite_mcp_server.db` | MCP database - empty or will be recreated |
| `.claude/` subfolder | Local settings - harmless |

---

## Key Files Explained

### CLAUDE.md
Global instructions that apply to ALL projects. This is the "constitution" for Claude's behavior. Project-specific CLAUDE.md files in individual repos override or extend these.

### settings.json
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": ["Bash(npm run:*)", "WebSearch"],
    "deny": [],
    "defaultMode": "default"  // or "bypassPermissions"
  },
  "enabledPlugins": { ... },
  "alwaysThinkingEnabled": true
}
```

### commands/
Markdown files that define custom slash commands. File name becomes command name:
- `run.md` → `/run`
- `start-task.md` → `/start-task`

### skills/
Each skill folder contains:
- `SKILL.md` - Skill definition and instructions
- `references/` - Supporting documentation
- `scripts/` - Executable scripts (Python, JS)
- `assets/` - Templates, examples

---

## Copying to a New Machine

### Linux/Mac (rsync)
```bash
rsync -av --exclude='.claude.json' \
          --exclude='.credentials.json' \
          --exclude='projects/' \
          ~/.claude/ /path/to/new/location/.claude/
```

### Windows (PowerShell)
```powershell
robocopy "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude" "D:\new-location\.claude" /E /XF .claude.json .credentials.json /XD projects
```

### Windows (Command Prompt)
```cmd
xcopy "%USERPROFILE%\.claude" "D:\new-location\.claude" /E /I /EXCLUDE:exclude.txt
```
Where `exclude.txt` contains:
```
.claude.json
.credentials.json
\projects\
```

---

## After Copying

1. **Login**: Run `claude` - you'll need to authenticate on the new machine
2. **MCP Servers**: Check `.claude.json` for MCP config - may need Windows `cmd /c` wrappers
3. **Review settings.json**: Remove any machine-specific paths
4. **Install plugins**: Run `/plugin install` for any plugins you need

---

## Maintenance

| Task | Frequency |
|------|-----------|
| Clear `plans/` | Monthly (session-specific, safe to delete) |
| Clear `history.jsonl` | As needed (can grow to several MB) |
| Clear `debug/` | As needed |
| Update skills | When new versions available |
| Review CLAUDE.md | Quarterly or after major project changes |

---

## Plugin Marketplaces

Plugins are installed from marketplaces and cached in `plugins/cache/`. Current enabled:
- `claude-code-plugins` - Official Anthropic plugins
- `anthropic-agent-skills` - Document skills (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX)
- `taches-cc-resources` - Community resources

---

*Last updated: December 2024*

-2. **MCP Servers**: Check `.claude.json` for MCP config - may need Windows `cmd /c` wrappers
-4. **Install plugins**: Run `/plugin install` for any plugins you need
+2. **MCP Servers**: Configure MCP servers in the new `.claude.json` (Windows needs `cmd /c` wrappers for npx)
+3. **Review settings.json**: Check for any machine-specific paths in permissions

## DO NOT COPY (Only 3 items)

| Item | Reason |

message.content: Everything is not supposed to have machine specific location paths, it should all just be setup to know .claude.json is above it and this folder itself can exist anywhere. How can we accomplish that? I also keep a replica of .claude.json inside of this folder for easier copying to make sure it doesn't get left behind - we can utilize that aspect too.

content.thinking:
The user wants to make the entire `.claude/` folder completely portable, including having a template `.claude.json` inside it. The idea is:

1. The `.claude/` folder can be copied anywhere
2. A template `.claude.json` is kept inside the folder (maybe as `.claude.json.template` or `claude.json.template`)
3. When setting up on a new machine, you copy the template to the parent directory and modify as needed

Let me look at what's in their current `.claude.json` to understand what needs to be templated vs regenerated:

From earlier, I saw `.claude.json` contains:
- `mcpServers` - MCP server config (portable after we fixed the cmd /c issue)
- OAuth account info (machine-specific, regenerated on login)
- `projects` - path-keyed project data (machine-specific)
- Various caches and stats (regenerated)

The user wants to keep a replica inside the folder. Let me check if there's already one there.

Looking at the earlier listing:
```
-rw-r--r-- 1 tjn 197121   52455 Dec 13 19:37 .claude.json
```

This is in the root of `~/.claude/`. Let me check if there's also one inside `~/.claude/.claude/`:
```
drwxr-xr-x 1 tjn 197121       0 Dec 13 15:00 .claude
```

And from the find output:
```
C:/Users/tjn/.claude/.claude/settings.local.json
C:/Users/tjn/.claude/.claude.json
```

So there IS a `.claude.json` inside the `.claude/` folder already. That's the replica the user mentioned.

The goal is:
1. Make the folder completely self-contained
2. The `.claude.json` inside can be used as the source when copying
3. Update README to explain this workflow

Let me think about how Claude Code actually finds its config:


Claude Code searches for configuration in specific locations, with a preference for local project settings and a fallback to global user settings. The application will first check the current project's `.claude/` directory, then fall back to the user's home directory. This approach allows for flexible, context-aware configuration management across different machines and projects.

When setting up a new environment, users can simply:
1. Copy the entire `.claude/` folder to their home directory
2. Use the internal `.claude.json` as a starting template
3. Modify machine-specific settings as needed

The portable structure ensures easy migration and consistent configuration across different development environments.

## 2.0.69

- Minor bugfixes

## 2.0.68

- Fixed IME (Input Method Editor) support for languages like Chinese, Japanese, and Korean by correctly positioning the composition window at the cursor
- Fixed a bug where disallowed MCP tools were visible to the model
- Fixed an issue where steering messages could be lost while a subagent is working
- Fixed Option+Arrow word navigation treating entire CJK (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) text sequences as a single word instead of navigating by word boundaries
- Improved plan mode exit UX: show simplified yes/no dialog when exiting with empty or missing plan instead of throwing an error
- Add support for enterprise managed settings. Contact your Anthropic account team to enable this feature.

## 2.0.67

- Thinking mode is now enabled by default for Opus 4.5
- Thinking mode configuration has moved to /config
- Added search functionality to `/permissions` command with `/` keyboard shortcut for filtering rules by tool name
- Show reason why autoupdater is disabled in `/doctor`
- Fixed false "Another process is currently updating Claude" error when running `claude update` while another instance is already on the latest version
- Fixed MCP servers from `.mcp.json` being stuck in pending state when running in non-interactive mode (`-p` flag or piped input)
- Fixed scroll position resetting after deleting a permission rule in `/permissions`
- Fixed word deletion (opt+delete) and word navigation (opt+arrow) not working correctly with non-Latin text such as Cyrillic, Greek, Arabic, Hebrew, Thai, and Chinese
- Fixed `claude install --force` not bypassing stale lock files
- Fixed consecutive @~/ file references in CLAUDE.md being incorrectly parsed due to markdown strikethrough interference
- Windows: Fixed plugin MCP servers failing due to colons in log directory paths

## 2.0.65

- Added ability to switch models while writing a prompt using alt+p (linux, windows), option+p (macos).
- Added context window information to status line input
- Added `fileSuggestion` setting for custom `@` file search commands
- Added `CLAUDE_CODE_SHELL` environment variable to override automatic shell detection (useful when login shell differs from actual working shell)
- Fixed prompt not being saved to history when aborting a query with Escape
- Fixed Read tool image handling to identify format from bytes instead of file extension

## 2.0.64

- Made auto-compacting instant
- Agents and bash commands can run asynchronously and send messages to wake up the main agent
- /stats now provides users with interesting CC stats, such as favorite model, usage graph, usage streak
- Added named session support: use `/rename` to name sessions, `/resume <name>` in REPL or `claude --resume <name>` from the terminal to resume them
- Added support for .claude/rules/`.  See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory for details.
- Added image dimension metadata when images are resized, enabling accurate coordinate mappings for large images
- Fixed auto-loading .env when using native installer
- Fixed `--system-prompt` being ignored when using `--continue` or `--resume` flags
- Improved `/resume` screen with grouped forked sessions and keyboard shortcuts for preview (P) and rename (R)
- VSCode: Added copy-to-clipboard button on code blocks and bash tool inputs
- VSCode: Fixed extension not working on Windows ARM64 by falling back to x64 binary via emulation
- Bedrock: Improve efficiency of token counting
- Bedrock: Add support for `aws login` AWS Management Console credentials
- Unshipped AgentOutputTool and BashOutputTool, in favor of a new unified TaskOutputTool

## 2.0.62

- Added "(Recommended)" indicator for multiple-choice questions, with the recommended option moved to the top of the list
- Added `attribution` setting to customize commit and PR bylines (deprecates `includeCoAuthoredBy`)
- Fixed duplicate slash commands appearing when ~/.claude is symlinked to a project directory
- Fixed slash command selection not working when multiple commands share the same name
- Fixed an issue where skill files inside symlinked skill directories could become circular symlinks
- Fixed running versions getting removed because lock file incorrectly going stale
- Fixed IDE diff tab not closing when rejecting file changes

## 2.0.61

- Reverted VSCode support for multiple terminal clients due to responsiveness issues.

- Added background agent support. Agents run in the background while you work

- Enabled "/mcp enable [server-name]" or "/mcp disable [server-name]" to quickly toggle all servers
- Updated Fetch to skip summarization for pre-approved websites
- VSCode: Added support for multiple terminal clients connecting to the IDE server simultaneously

## 2.0.59

- Added --agent CLI flag to override the agent setting for the current session

- Fix: Remove trailing newlines from bash output in terminal wrapping

## 1.0.56

- Windows: Enabled shift+tab for mode switching on versions of Node.js that support terminal VT mode
- Fixes for WSL IDE detection
- Fix an issue causing awsRefreshHelper changes to .aws directory not to be picked up

## 1.0.55

- Clarified knowledge cutoff for Opus 4 and Sonnet 4 models
- Windows: fixed Ctrl+Z crash
- SDK: Added ability to capture error logging
- Add --system-prompt-file option to override system prompt in print mode

## 1.0.54

- Hooks: Added UserPromptSubmit hook and the current working directory to hook inputs
- Custom slash commands: Added argument-hint to frontmatter
- Windows: OAuth uses port 45454 and properly constructs browser URL
- Windows: mode switching now uses alt + m, and plan mode renders properly
- Shell: Switch to in-memory shell snapshot to fix file-related errors

## 1.0.53

- Updated @-mention file truncation from 100 lines to 2000 lines
- Add helper script settings for AWS token refresh: awsAuthRefresh (for foreground operations like aws sso login) and awsCredentialExport (for background operation with STS-like response).

## 1.0.52

- Added support for MCP server instructions

## 1.0.51

- Added support for native Windows (requires Git for Windows)
- Added support for Bedrock API keys through environment variable AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK
- Settings: /doctor can now help you identify and fix invalid setting files
- `--append-system-prompt` can now be used in interactive mode, not just --print/-p.
- Increased auto-compact warning threshold from 60% to 80%
- Fixed an issue with handling user directories with spaces for shell snapshots
- OTEL resource now includes os.type, os.version, host.arch, and wsl.version (if running on Windows Subsystem for Linux)
- Custom slash commands: Fixed user-level commands in subdirectories
- Plan mode: Fixed issue where rejected plan from sub-task would get discarded

## 1.0.48

- Fixed a bug in v1.0.45 where the app would sometimes freeze on launch
- Added progress messages to Bash tool based on the last 5 lines of command output
- Added expanding variables support for MCP server configuration
- Moved shell snapshots from /tmp to ~/.claude for more reliable Bash tool calls
- Improved IDE extension path handling when Claude Code runs in WSL
- Hooks: Added a PreCompact hook
- Vim mode: Added c, f/F, t/T

## 1.0.45

- Redesigned Search (Grep) tool with new tool input parameters and features
- Disabled IDE diffs for notebook files, fixing "Timeout waiting after 1000ms" error
- Fixed config file corruption issue by enforcing atomic writes
- Updated prompt input undo to Ctrl+\_ to avoid breaking existing Ctrl+U behavior, matching zsh's undo shortcut
- Stop Hooks: Fixed transcript path after /clear and fixed triggering when loop ends with tool call
- Custom slash commands: Restored namespacing in command names based on subdirectories. For example, .claude/commands/frontend/component.md is now /frontend:component, not /component.

## 1.0.44

- New /export command lets you quickly export a conversation for sharing
- MCP: resource_link tool results are now supported
- MCP: tool annotations and tool titles now display in /mcp view
- Changed Ctrl+Z to suspend Claude Code. Resume by running `fg`. Prompt input undo is now Ctrl+U.

## 1.0.43

- Fixed a bug where the theme selector was saving excessively
- Hooks: Added EPIPE system error handling

## 1.0.42

- Added tilde (`~`) expansion support to `/add-dir` command

## 1.0.41

- Hooks: Split Stop hook triggering into Stop and SubagentStop
- Hooks: Enabled optional timeout configuration for each command
- Hooks: Added "hook_event_name" to hook input
- Fixed a bug where MCP tools would display twice in tool list
- New tool parameters JSON for Bash tool in `tool_decision` event

## 1.0.40

- Fixed a bug causing API connection errors with UNABLE_TO_GET_ISSUER_CERT_LOCALLY if `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` was set

## 1.0.39

- New Active Time metric in OpenTelemetry logging

## 1.0.38

- Released hooks. Special thanks to community input in https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/712. Docs: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks

## 1.0.37

- Remove ability to set `Proxy-Authorization` header via ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN or apiKeyHelper

## 1.0.36

- Web search now takes today's date into context
- Fixed a bug where stdio MCP servers were not terminating properly on exit

## 1.0.35

- Added support for MCP OAuth Authorization Server discovery

## 1.0.34

- Fixed a memory leak causing a MaxListenersExceededWarning message to appear

## 1.0.33

- Improved logging functionality with session ID support
- Added prompt input undo functionality (Ctrl+Z and vim 'u' command)
- Improvements to plan mode

## 1.0.32

- Updated loopback config for litellm
- Added forceLoginMethod setting to bypass login selection screen

## 1.0.31

- Fixed a bug where ~/.claude.json would get reset when file contained invalid JSON

## 1.0.30

- Custom slash commands: Run bash output, @-mention files, enable thinking with thinking keywords
- Improved file path autocomplete with filename matching
- Added timestamps in Ctrl-r mode and fixed Ctrl-c handling
- Enhanced jq regex support for complex filters with pipes and select

## 1.0.29

- Improved CJK character support in cursor navigation and rendering

## 1.0.28

- Slash commands: Fix selector display during history navigation
- Resizes images before upload to prevent API size limit errors
- Added XDG_CONFIG_HOME support to configuration directory
- Performance optimizations for memory usage
- New attributes (terminal.type, language) in OpenTelemetry logging

## 1.0.27

- Streamable HTTP MCP servers are now supported
- Remote MCP servers (SSE and HTTP) now support OAuth
- MCP resources can now be @-mentioned
- /resume slash command to switch conversations within Claude Code

## 1.0.25

- Slash commands: moved "project" and "user" prefixes to descriptions
- Slash commands: improved reliability for command discovery
- Improved support for Ghostty
- Improved web search reliability

## 1.0.24

- Improved /mcp output
- Fixed a bug where settings arrays got overwritten instead of merged

## 1.0.23

- Released TypeScript SDK: import @anthropic-ai/claude-code to get started
- Released Python SDK: pip install claude-code-sdk to get started

## 1.0.22

- SDK: Renamed `total_cost` to `total_cost_usd`

## 1.0.21

- Improved editing of files with tab-based indentation
- Fix for tool_use without matching tool_result errors
- Fixed a bug where stdio MCP server processes would linger after quitting Claude Code

## 1.0.18

- Added --add-dir CLI argument for specifying additional working directories
- Added streaming input support without require -p flag
- Improved startup performance and session storage performance
- Added CLAUDE_BASH_MAINTAIN_PROJECT_WORKING_DIR environment variable to freeze working directory for bash commands
- Added detailed MCP server tools display (/mcp)
- MCP authentication and permission improvements
- Added auto-reconnection for MCP SSE connections on disconnect
- Fixed issue where pasted content was lost when dialogs appeared

## 1.0.17

- We now emit messages from sub-tasks in -p mode (look for the parent_tool_use_id property)
- Fixed crashes when the VS Code diff tool is invoked multiple times quickly
- MCP server list UI improvements
- Update Claude Code process title to display "claude" instead of "node"

## 1.0.11

- Claude Code can now also be used with a Claude Pro subscription
- Added /upgrade for smoother switching to Claude Max plans
- Improved UI for authentication from API keys and Bedrock/Vertex/external auth tokens
- Improved shell configuration error handling
- Improved todo list handling during compaction

## 1.0.10

- Added markdown table support
- Improved streaming performance

## 1.0.8

- Fixed Vertex AI region fallback when using CLOUD_ML_REGION
- Increased default otel interval from 1s -> 5s
- Fixed edge cases where MCP_TIMEOUT and MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT weren't being respected
- Fixed a regression where search tools unnecessarily asked for permissions
- Added support for triggering thinking non-English languages
- Improved compacting UI

## 1.0.7

- Renamed /allowed-tools -> /permissions
- Migrated allowedTools and ignorePatterns from .claude.json -> settings.json
- Deprecated claude config commands in favor of editing settings.json
- Fixed a bug where --dangerously-skip-permissions sometimes didn't work in --print mode
- Improved error handling for /install-github-app
- Bugfixes, UI polish, and tool reliability improvements

## 1.0.6

- Improved edit reliability for tab-indented files
- Respect CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR everywhere
- Reduced unnecessary tool permission prompts
- Added support for symlinks in @file typeahead
- Bugfixes, UI polish, and tool reliability improvements

## 1.0.4

- Fixed a bug where MCP tool errors weren't being parsed correctly

## 1.0.1

- Added `DISABLE_INTERLEAVED_THINKING` to give users the option to opt out of interleaved thinking.
- Improved model references to show provider-specific names (Sonnet 3.7 for Bedrock, Sonnet 4 for Console)
- Updated documentation links and OAuth process descriptions

## 1.0.0

- Claude Code is now generally available
- Introducing Sonnet 4 and Opus 4 models

## 0.2.125

- Breaking change: Bedrock ARN passed to `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` or `ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL` should no longer contain an escaped slash (specify `/` instead of `%2F`)
- Removed `DEBUG=true` in favor of `ANTHROPIC_LOG=debug`, to log all requests

## 0.2.117

- Breaking change: --print JSON output now returns nested message objects, for forwards-compatibility as we introduce new metadata fields
- Introduced settings.cleanupPeriodDays
- Introduced CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER_TTL_MS env var
- Introduced --debug mode

## 0.2.108

- You can now send messages to Claude while it works to steer Claude in real-time
- Introduced BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS and BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS env vars
- Fixed a bug where thinking was not working in -p mode
- Fixed a regression in /cost reporting
- Deprecated MCP wizard interface in favor of other MCP commands
- Lots of other bugfixes and improvements

## 0.2.107

- CLAUDE.md files can now import other files. Add @path/to/file.md to ./CLAUDE.md to load additional files on launch

## 0.2.106

- MCP SSE server configs can now specify custom headers
- Fixed a bug where MCP permission prompt didn't always show correctly

## 0.2.105

- Claude can now search the web
- Moved system & account status to /status
- Added word movement keybindings for Vim
- Improved latency for startup, todo tool, and file edits

## 0.2.102

- Improved thinking triggering reliability
- Improved @mention reliability for images and folders
- You can now paste multiple large chunks into one prompt

## 0.2.100

- Fixed a crash caused by a stack overflow error
- Made db storage optional; missing db support disables --continue and --resume

## 0.2.98

- Fixed an issue where auto-compact was running twice

## 0.2.96

- Claude Code can now also be used with a Claude Max subscription (https://claude.ai/upgrade)

## 0.2.93

- Resume conversations from where you left off from with "claude --continue" and "claude --resume"
- Claude now has access to a Todo list that helps it stay on track and be more organized

## 0.2.82

- Added support for --disallowedTools
- Renamed tools for consistency: LSTool -> LS, View -> Read, etc.

## 0.2.75

- Hit Enter to queue up additional messages while Claude is working
- Drag in or copy/paste image files directly into the prompt
- @-mention files to directly add them to context
- Run one-off MCP servers with `claude --mcp-config <path-to-file>`
- Improved performance for filename auto-complete

## 0.2.74

- Added support for refreshing dynamically generated API keys (via apiKeyHelper), with a 5 minute TTL
- Task tool can now perform writes and run bash commands

## 0.2.72

- Updated spinner to indicate tokens loaded and tool usage

## 0.2.70

- Network commands like curl are now available for Claude to use
- Claude can now run multiple web queries in parallel
- Pressing ESC once immediately interrupts Claude in Auto-accept mode

## 0.2.69

- Fixed UI glitches with improved Select component behavior
- Enhanced terminal output display with better text truncation logic

## 0.2.67

- Shared project permission rules can be saved in .claude/settings.json

## 0.2.66

- Print mode (-p) now supports streaming output via --output-format=stream-json
- Fixed issue where pasting could trigger memory or bash mode unexpectedly

## 0.2.63

- Fixed an issue where MCP tools were loaded twice, which caused tool call errors

## 0.2.61

- Navigate menus with vim-style keys (j/k) or bash/emacs shortcuts (Ctrl+n/p) for faster interaction
- Enhanced image detection for more reliable clipboard paste functionality
- Fixed an issue where ESC key could crash the conversation history selector

## 0.2.59

- Copy+paste images directly into your prompt
- Improved progress indicators for bash and fetch tools
- Bugfixes for non-interactive mode (-p)

## 0.2.54

- Quickly add to Memory by starting your message with '#'
- Press ctrl+r to see full output for long tool results
- Added support for MCP SSE transport

## 0.2.53

- New web fetch tool lets Claude view URLs that you paste in
- Fixed a bug with JPEG detection

## 0.2.50

- New MCP "project" scope now allows you to add MCP servers to .mcp.json files and commit them to your repository

## 0.2.49

- Previous MCP server scopes have been renamed: previous "project" scope is now "local" and "global" scope is now "user"

## 0.2.47

- Press Tab to auto-complete file and folder names
- Press Shift + Tab to toggle auto-accept for file edits
- Automatic conversation compaction for infinite conversation length (toggle with /config)

## 0.2.44

- Ask Claude to make a plan with thinking mode: just say 'think' or 'think harder' or even 'ultrathink'

## 0.2.41

- MCP server startup timeout can now be configured via MCP_TIMEOUT environment variable
- MCP server startup no longer blocks the app from starting up

## 0.2.37

- New /release-notes command lets you view release notes at any time
- `claude config add/remove` commands now accept multiple values separated by commas or spaces

## 0.2.36

- Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop with `claude mcp add-from-claude-desktop`
- Add MCP servers as JSON strings with `claude mcp add-json <n> <json>`

## 0.2.34

- Vim bindings for text input - enable with /vim or /config

## 0.2.32

- Interactive MCP setup wizard: Run "claude mcp add" to add MCP servers with a step-by-step interface
- Fix for some PersistentShell issues

## 0.2.31

- Custom slash commands: Markdown files in .claude/commands/ directories now appear as custom slash commands to insert prompts into your conversation
- MCP debug mode: Run with --mcp-debug flag to get more information about MCP server errors

## 0.2.30

- Added ANSI color theme for better terminal compatibility
- Fixed issue where slash command arguments weren't being sent properly
- (Mac-only) API keys are now stored in macOS Keychain

## 0.2.26

- New /approved-tools command for managing tool permissions
- Word-level diff display for improved code readability
- Fuzzy matching for slash commands

## 0.2.21

- Fuzzy matching for /commands

  "cachedChangelog": "# Changelog

## 2.0.69

- Minor bugfixes

## 2.0.68

- Fixed IME (Input Method Editor) support for languages like Chinese, Japanese, and Korean by correctly positioning the composition window at the cursor
- Fixed a bug where disallowed MCP tools were visible to the model
- Fixed an issue where steering messages could be lost while a subagent is working
- Fixed Option+Arrow word navigation treating entire CJK (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) text sequences as a single word instead of navigating by word boundaries
- Improved plan mode exit UX: show simplified yes/no dialog when exiting with empty or missing plan instead of throwing an error
- Add support for enterprise managed settings. Contact your Anthropic account team to enable this feature.

## 2.0.67

- Thinking mode is now enabled by default for Opus 4.5
- Thinking mode configuration has moved to /config
- Added search functionality to `/permissions` command with `/` keyboard shortcut for filtering rules by tool name
- Show reason why autoupdater is disabled in `/doctor`
- Fixed false "Another process is currently updating Claude" error when running `claude update` while another instance is already on the latest version
- Fixed MCP servers from `.mcp.json` being stuck in pending state when running in non-interactive mode (`-p` flag or piped input)
- Fixed scroll position resetting after deleting a permission rule in `/permissions`
- Fixed word deletion (opt+delete) and word navigation (opt+arrow) not working correctly with non-Latin text such as Cyrillic, Greek, Arabic, Hebrew, Thai, and Chinese
- Fixed `claude install --force` not bypassing stale lock files
- Fixed consecutive @~/ file references in CLAUDE.md being incorrectly parsed due to markdown strikethrough interference
- Windows: Fixed plugin MCP servers failing due to colons in log directory paths

## 2.0.65

- Added ability to switch models while writing a prompt using alt+p (linux, windows), option+p (macos).
- Added context window information to status line input
- Added `fileSuggestion` setting for custom `@` file search commands
- Added `CLAUDE_CODE_SHELL` environment variable to override automatic shell detection (useful when login shell differs from actual working shell)
- Fixed prompt not being saved to history when aborting a query with Escape
- Fixed Read tool image handling to identify format from bytes instead of file extension

## 2.0.64

- Made auto-compacting instant
- Agents and bash commands can run asynchronously and send messages to wake up the main agent
- /stats now provides users with interesting CC stats, such as favorite model, usage graph, usage streak
- Added named session support: use `/rename` to name sessions, `/resume <name>` in REPL or `claude --resume <name>` from the terminal to resume them
- Added support for .claude/rules/`.  See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory for details.
- Added image dimension metadata when images are resized, enabling accurate coordinate mappings for large images
- Fixed auto-loading .env when using native installer
- Fixed `--system-prompt` being ignored when using `--continue` or `--resume` flags
- Improved `/resume` screen with grouped forked sessions and keyboard shortcuts for preview (P) and rename (R)
- VSCode: Added copy-to-clipboard button on code blocks and bash tool inputs
- VSCode: Fixed extension not working on Windows ARM64 by falling back to x64 binary via emulation
- Bedrock: Improve efficiency of token counting
- Bedrock: Add support for `aws login` AWS Management Console credentials
- Unshipped AgentOutputTool and BashOutputTool, in favor of a new unified TaskOutputTool

## 2.0.62

- Added "(Recommended)" indicator for multiple-choice questions, with the recommended option moved to the top of the list
- Added `attribution` setting to customize commit and PR bylines (deprecates `includeCoAuthoredBy`)
- Fixed duplicate slash commands appearing when ~/.claude is symlinked to a project directory
- Fixed slash command selection not working when multiple commands share the same name
- Fixed an issue where skill files inside symlinked skill directories could become circular symlinks
- Fixed running versions getting removed because lock file incorrectly going stale
- Fixed IDE diff tab not closing when rejecting file changes

## 2.0.61

- Reverted VSCode support for multiple terminal clients due to responsiveness issues.

## 2.0.60

- Added background agent support. Agents run in the background while you work
- Added --disable-slash-commands CLI flag to disable all slash commands
- Added model name to "Co-Authored-By" commit messages
- Enabled "/mcp enable [server-name]" or "/mcp disable [server-name]" to quickly toggle all servers
- Updated Fetch to skip summarization for pre-approved websites
- VSCode: Added support for multiple terminal clients connecting to the IDE server simultaneously

## 2.0.59

- Added --agent CLI flag to override the agent setting for the current session
- Added `agent` setting to configure main thread with a specific agent's system prompt, tool restrictions, and model
- VS Code: Fixed .claude.json config file being read from incorrect location

## 2.0.58

- Pro users now have access to Opus 4.5 as part of their subscription!
- Fixed timer duration showing "11m 60s" instead of "12m 0s"
- Windows: Managed settings now prefer `C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode` if it exists. Support for `C:\ProgramData\ClaudeCode` will be removed in a future version.

## 2.0.57

- Added feedback input when rejecting plans, allowing users to tell Claude what to change
- VSCode: Added streaming message support for real-time response display

## 2.0.56

- Added setting to enable/disable terminal progress bar (OSC 9;4)
- VSCode Extension: Added support for VS Code's secondary sidebar (VS Code 1.97+), allowing Claude Code to be displayed in the right sidebar while keeping the file explorer on the left. Requires setting sidebar as Preferred Location in the config.

## 2.0.55

- Fixed proxy DNS resolution being forced on by default. Now opt-in via `CLAUDE_CODE_PROXY_RESOLVES_HOSTS=true` environment variable
- Fixed keyboard navigation becoming unresponsive when holding down arrow keys in memory location selector

- Improved editing of files with tab-based indentation
- Fix for tool_use without matching tool_result errors
- Fixed a bug where stdio MCP server processes would linger after quitting Claude Code

## 1.0.18

- Added --add-dir CLI argument for specifying additional working directories
- Added streaming input support without require -p flag
- Improved startup performance and session storage performance
- Added CLAUDE_BASH_MAINTAIN_PROJECT_WORKING_DIR environment variable to freeze working directory for bash commands
- Added detailed MCP server tools display (/mcp)
- MCP authentication and permission improvements
- Added auto-reconnection for MCP SSE connections on disconnect
- Fixed issue where pasted content was lost when dialogs appeared

## 1.0.17

- We now emit messages from sub-tasks in -p mode (look for the parent_tool_use_id property)
- Fixed crashes when the VS Code diff tool is invoked multiple times quickly
- MCP server list UI improvements
- Update Claude Code process title to display "claude" instead of "node"

## 1.0.11

- Claude Code can now also be used with a Claude Pro subscription
- Added /upgrade for smoother switching to Claude Max plans
- Improved UI for authentication from API keys and Bedrock/Vertex/external auth tokens
- Improved shell configuration error handling
- Improved todo list handling during compaction

## 1.0.10

- Added markdown table support
- Improved streaming performance

## 1.0.8

- Fixed Vertex AI region fallback when using CLOUD_ML_REGION
- Increased default otel interval from 1s -> 5s
- Fixed edge cases where MCP_TIMEOUT and MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT weren't being respected
- Fixed a regression where search tools unnecessarily asked for permissions
- Added support for triggering thinking non-English languages
- Improved compacting UI

## 1.0.7

- Renamed /allowed-tools -> /permissions
- Migrated allowedTools and ignorePatterns from .claude.json -> settings.json
- Deprecated claude config commands in favor of editing settings.json
- Fixed a bug where --dangerously-skip-permissions sometimes didn't work in --print mode
- Improved error handling for /install-github-app
- Bugfixes, UI polish, and tool reliability improvements

## 1.0.6

- Improved edit reliability for tab-indented files
- Respect CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR everywhere
- Reduced unnecessary tool permission prompts
- Added support for symlinks in @file typeahead
- Bugfixes, UI polish, and tool reliability improvements

## 1.0.4

- Fixed a bug where MCP tool errors weren't being parsed correctly

## 1.0.1

- Added `DISABLE_INTERLEAVED_THINKING` to give users the option to opt out of interleaved thinking.
- Improved model references to show provider-specific names (Sonnet 3.7 for Bedrock, Sonnet 4 for Console)
- Updated documentation links and OAuth process descriptions

## 1.0.0

- Claude Code is now generally available
- Introducing Sonnet 4 and Opus 4 models

## 0.2.125

- Breaking change: Bedrock ARN passed to `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` or `ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL` should no longer contain an escaped slash (specify `/` instead of `%2F`)
- Removed `DEBUG=true` in favor of `ANTHROPIC_LOG=debug`, to log all requests

## 0.2.117

- Breaking change: --print JSON output now returns nested message objects, for forwards-compatibility as we introduce new metadata fields
- Introduced settings.cleanupPeriodDays
- Introduced CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER_TTL_MS env var
- Introduced --debug mode

## 0.2.108

- You can now send messages to Claude while it works to steer Claude in real-time
- Introduced BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS and BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS env vars
- Fixed a bug where thinking was not working in -p mode
- Fixed a regression in /cost reporting
- Deprecated MCP wizard interface in favor of other MCP commands
- Lots of other bugfixes and improvements

## 0.2.107

- CLAUDE.md files can now import other files. Add @path/to/file.md to ./CLAUDE.md to load additional files on launch

## 0.2.106

- MCP SSE server configs can now specify custom headers
- Fixed a bug where MCP permission prompt didn't always show correctly

## 0.2.105

- Claude can now search the web
- Moved system & account status to /status
- Added word movement keybindings for Vim
- Improved latency for startup, todo tool, and file edits

## 0.2.102

- Improved thinking triggering reliability
- Improved @mention reliability for images and folders
- You can now paste multiple large chunks into one prompt

## 0.2.100

- Fixed a crash caused by a stack overflow error
- Made db storage optional; missing db support disables --continue and --resume

## 0.2.98

- Fixed an issue where auto-compact was running twice

## 0.2.96

- Claude Code can now also be used with a Claude Max subscription (https://claude.ai/upgrade)

## 0.2.93

- Resume conversations from where you left off from with "claude --continue" and "claude --resume"
- Claude now has access to a Todo list that helps it stay on track and be more organized

## 0.2.82

- Added support for --disallowedTools
- Renamed tools for consistency: LSTool -> LS, View -> Read, etc.

## 0.2.75

- Hit Enter to queue up additional messages while Claude is working
- Drag in or copy/paste image files directly into the prompt
- @-mention files to directly add them to context
- Run one-off MCP servers with `claude --mcp-config <path-to-file>`
- Improved performance for filename auto-complete

## 0.2.74

- Added support for refreshing dynamically generated API keys (via apiKeyHelper), with a 5 minute TTL
- Task tool can now perform writes and run bash commands

## 0.2.72

- Updated spinner to indicate tokens loaded and tool usage

## 0.2.70

- Network commands like curl are now available for Claude to use
- Claude can now run multiple web queries in parallel
- Pressing ESC once immediately interrupts Claude in Auto-accept mode

## 0.2.69

- Fixed UI glitches with improved Select component behavior
- Enhanced terminal output display with better text truncation logic

## 0.2.67

- Shared project permission rules can be saved in .claude/settings.json

## 0.2.66

- Print mode (-p) now supports streaming output via --output-format=stream-json
- Fixed issue where pasting could trigger memory or bash mode unexpectedly

## 0.2.63

- Fixed an issue where MCP tools were loaded twice, which caused tool call errors

## 0.2.61

- Navigate menus with vim-style keys (j/k) or bash/emacs shortcuts (Ctrl+n/p) for faster interaction
- Enhanced image detection for more reliable clipboard paste functionality
- Fixed an issue where ESC key could crash the conversation history selector

## 0.2.59

- Copy+paste images directly into your prompt
- Improved progress indicators for bash and fetch tools
- Bugfixes for non-interactive mode (-p)

## 0.2.54

- Quickly add to Memory by starting your message with '#'
- Press ctrl+r to see full output for long tool results
- Added support for MCP SSE transport

## 0.2.53

- New web fetch tool lets Claude view URLs that you paste in
- Fixed a bug with JPEG detection

## 0.2.50

- New MCP "project" scope now allows you to add MCP servers to .mcp.json files and commit them to your repository

## 0.2.49

- Previous MCP server scopes have been renamed: previous "project" scope is now "local" and "global" scope is now "user"

## 0.2.47

- Press Tab to auto-complete file and folder names
- Press Shift + Tab to toggle auto-accept for file edits
- Automatic conversation compaction for infinite conversation length (toggle with /config)

## 0.2.44

- Ask Claude to make a plan with thinking mode: just say 'think' or 'think harder' or even 'ultrathink'

## 0.2.41

- MCP server startup timeout can now be configured via MCP_TIMEOUT environment variable
- MCP server startup no longer blocks the app from starting up

## 0.2.37

- New /release-notes command lets you view release notes at any time
- `claude config add/remove` commands now accept multiple values separated by commas or spaces

## 0.2.36

- Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop with `claude mcp add-from-claude-desktop`
- Add MCP servers as JSON strings with `claude mcp add-json <n> <json>`

## 0.2.34

- Vim bindings for text input - enable with /vim or /config

## 0.2.32

- Interactive MCP setup wizard: Run "claude mcp add" to add MCP servers with a step-by-step interface
- Fix for some PersistentShell issues

## 0.2.31

- Custom slash commands: Markdown files in .claude/commands/ directories now appear as custom slash commands to insert prompts into your conversation
- MCP debug mode: Run with --mcp-debug flag to get more information about MCP server errors

## 0.2.30

- Added ANSI color theme for better terminal compatibility
- Fixed issue where slash command arguments weren't being sent properly
- (Mac-only) API keys are now stored in macOS Keychain

## 0.2.26

- New /approved-tools command for managing tool permissions
- Word-level diff display for improved code readability
- Fuzzy matching for slash commands

## 0.2.21

Or alternatively:
2. Keep the full copy and document that after copying, you need to:

   - The projects section will be ignored/overwritten as you use new projects

content.text: Now let me update the README to explain this self-contained workflow:

input.content:
# .claude/ Directory

This folder is **fully portable**. Copy it anywhere and it works.

---

## Quick Setup on New Machine

```powershell
# 1. Copy entire folder to new location
cp -r /path/to/.claude ~/.claude

# 2. Copy the template to parent directory
cp ~/.claude/claude.json.template ~/.claude.json

# 3. Run claude and login
claude
```

That's it. Claude Code will populate OAuth tokens and other runtime data on first login.

---

## Folder Structure

```
.claude/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── CLAUDE.md                 # Global instructions (your "constitution")
├── claude.json.template      # ← PORTABLE: Copy to parent as .claude.json
│
├── settings.json             # Permissions, plugins, preferences
├── settings.local.json       # Local overrides
│
├── commands/                 # Custom slash commands
│   ├── run.md               # /run
│   └── start-task.md        # /start-task
│
├── skills/                   # Reusable skills
│   ├── _shared/             # Shared standards
│   ├── generic-*/           # Stack-agnostic skills
│   ├── codebase-documenter/
│   ├── frontend-enhancer/
│   ├── tech-debt-analyzer/
│   ├── test-specialist/
│   └── ...
│
├── templates/                # Task templates
├── docs/                     # Documentation
├── CLAUDE.md Reference/      # Reference files from other projects
│
├── .claude.json              # Full backup (contains auth - regenerate on new machine)
├── .credentials.json         # Auth credentials (regenerated on login)
│
├── history.jsonl             # Conversation history
├── plans/                    # Session plan files
├── todos/                    # Todo states
├── debug/                    # Debug logs
│
├── projects/                 # Per-project state (auto-generated per path)
├── plugins/                  # Downloaded plugins
├── file-history/             # File change tracking
├── shell-snapshots/          # Bash state
├── statsig/                  # Feature flags
├── stats-cache.json          # Usage stats
├── telemetry/                # Telemetry
├── ide/                      # IDE connection
├── downloads/                # Temp files
└── sqlite_mcp_server.db      # MCP database
```

---

## Key Files

### claude.json.template
**This is the portable config.** Contains only:
- MCP server definitions (with Windows `cmd /c` wrappers)
- No OAuth tokens (regenerated on login)
- No path-specific data

Copy this to `~/.claude.json` on any new machine.

### .claude.json (internal backup)
Full copy of the active config. Contains OAuth tokens and path-encoded project data.
Useful as a backup but **will need re-authentication** on a new machine.

### CLAUDE.md
Your global instructions that apply to all projects. This is the "constitution" for Claude's behavior.

### settings.json
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": ["Bash(npm run:*)", "WebSearch", ...],
    "deny": [],
    "defaultMode": "default"
  },
  "enabledPlugins": { ... },
  "alwaysThinkingEnabled": true
}
```

### commands/
Markdown files → slash commands. Filename = command name.

### skills/
Each skill has:
- `SKILL.md` - Definition and instructions
- `references/` - Supporting docs
- `scripts/` - Executable scripts
- `assets/` - Templates, examples

---

## Copying to New Machine

### Full Copy (Recommended)
```powershell
# Copy everything
robocopy "C:\Users\tjn\.claude" "D:\new-machine\.claude" /E

# Create the parent config from template
copy "D:\new-machine\.claude\claude.json.template" "D:\new-machine\.claude.json"

# Login to regenerate auth
claude
```

### What Happens After Copy
| Item | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| `claude.json.template` | You copy to parent as `.claude.json` |
| MCP servers | Work immediately (paths are relative/global) |
| OAuth tokens | Regenerated on first `claude` login |
| `projects/` | Old paths ignored, new ones created as you work |
| `plugins/cache/` | Reused or regenerated as needed |
| `history.jsonl` | Your old conversations, viewable with `/resume` |

---

## Platform Notes

### Windows
MCP servers need `cmd /c` wrapper for npx commands. The template already has this.

### Linux/Mac
Change MCP server commands from:
```json
"command": "cmd",
"args": ["/c", "npx", ...]
```
To:
```json
"command": "npx",
"args": [...]
```

---

## Maintenance

| Task | When | Command |
|------|------|---------|
| Sync template with live config | After MCP changes | Copy `mcpServers` from `~/.claude.json` to `claude.json.template` |
| Clear old plans | Monthly | `rm -rf plans/*` |
| Clear history | When too large | `rm history.jsonl` |
| Clear debug logs | As needed | `rm -rf debug/*` |

---

## Sync Your MCP Servers

When you add/change MCP servers, update the template:

```powershell
# Extract just mcpServers from live config and update template
# (Or manually copy the mcpServers section)
```

Or simply keep `.claude.json` and `claude.json.template` in sync manually when you change MCP config.

---

*This folder is designed to be copied wholesale. Everything works relative to itself.*

---

## Folder Structure

```
.claude/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── CLAUDE.md                 # Global instructions (your "constitution")
├── claude.json.template      # ← PORTABLE: Copy to parent as .claude.json
│
├── settings.json             # Permissions, plugins, preferences
├── settings.local.json       # Local overrides
│
├── commands/                 # Custom slash commands
